summary: Hotz argues that AI coding assistants are fundamentally just compilers that take English as input rather than a traditional programming language. He contends that English is actually a poor choice for specifying programs due to its imprecision, non-determinism, and non-local effects. The perceived value of AI coding tools is really an indictment of how bad existing programming languages, compilers, and libraries are. He cites a study showing AI makes developers feel 20% more productive while actually making them 19% slower, and criticizes the billions wasted on 'vibe coding' companies just as billions were wasted on flawed self-driving approaches.
key_points: AI coding assistants are best understood as compilers that take English prompts as input and output code
English is a poor programming language: imprecise, non-deterministic, and changes have non-local effects on output
The success of LLMs for coding reflects how bad existing tooling, languages, and codebases are, not how good AI is
AI will replace programming jobs the same way compilers and spreadsheets replaced jobs—as tools, not magic
A study shows AI makes developers feel 20% more productive but actually makes them 19% slower
money_quotes: Most people do not care to find the truth, they care about what pumps their bags.
Do you believe a compiler can code? If so, then go right on believing that AI can code.
The best model of a programming AI is a compiler.
tl;dr, you think AI coding is good because compilers, languages, and libraries are bad.
That anyone uses LLMs to code is a testament to just how bad tooling and languages are.
That LLMs can replace developers at companies is a testament to how bad that company's codebase and hiring bar is.
Is your way of thinking so fucking broken that you can't believe anyone cares more about the actual truth than make believe dollars?
Or we could, you know, do the hard work and build better programming languages, compilers, and libraries. But that can't be hyped up for billions.
key_insight: AI coding tools are just compilers with English as the input language, and their perceived value says more about how bad our existing programming tools are than how good AI is.
